4:00 pm show-Roast Battle League LIVE in the Village Underground

Roast Battle League The Roast Battle League comes to The Comedy Cellar for the first time. Comics from all over the globe will go head-to-head with four battles lined up, nothing is off-limits, and no ego is safe. Come see the first sport that’s also a comedy show live in the Mecca of ball busting and trash talking. Let’s Roast!

Brian Moses CREATOR AND HOST OF "ROAST BATTLE"

6:00 pm show-Live with Nate Silver, Clare Malone and Galen Druke

Clare Malone staff writer at The New Yorker covering the media business, journalism, and politics. She previously wrote for FiveThirtyEight, where she reported on class, race, and electoral politics, and covered the 2016 and 2020 Presidential campaigns. Her writing has also appeared in New York, The Atlantic, GQ, The Columbia Journalism Review, and Harper’s.

Galen Druke founder and host of the GD POLITICS podcast. He previously hosted and produced the FiveThirtyEight Politics podcast, where he covered American elections for nearly a decade. His reporting and analysis have also regularly appeared on ABC News and the BBC. He began his career working for NPR affiliates in Wisconsin, New York City and Baltimore, where he attended The Johns Hopkins University. He is an executive member of the International Academy of Digital Arts and Sciences and lives in New York City.

Nate Silver American statistician, political analyst, author, sports gambler, and poker player who analyzes baseball, basketball and elections. He is the founder of FiveThirtyEight and held the position of editor-in-chief there, along with being a special correspondent for ABC News until May 2023. Since departing FiveThirtyEight, Silver has been publishing in his online newsletter Silver Bulletin and serves as an advisor to Polymarket

4:00 pm show-Golinsky Comedy Club Benefit Show

Golinsky Comedy Club n December 4, 2015, Dan Golinsky was hit by a reckless driver while on his nightly neighborhood walk, sustaining a traumatic brain injury that took his life eleven months later. His son Evan, then fourteen, founded the Golinsky Comedy Club to honor his father's love of stand-up — and to direct attention and funding toward traumatic brain injuries, a cause too often overlooked.In nearly a decade, the Comedy Club has raised over $120,000 for the Brain Trauma Foundation, Ryder Trauma Center at Jackson Memorial Hospital, and Achieve Miami

6:00 pm show-Sophie Gerson Healthy Youth Benefit Show

Sophie Gerson Healthy Youth Program Family, friends, and colleagues of Sophie Gerson founded Sophie Gerson Healthy Youth in June, 2013. Now, heading into its 10th year, SGHY has positively impacted the lives and futures of so many middle school students.About Our Mission To promote active lifestyles and the physical fitness of young people and to foster the values of good sportspersonship;To advance leadership and life skills through the common language of sports, dance, performing arts, and hands-on science, with special encouragement for girls;Using athletics, the arts, science, sociology and an integrated summer camp program, SGHY helps break the cycle of poverty by creating a sense of achievement, and reduce the incidence of bullying;To help the young adults understand that giving back to the community is a worthwhile endeavor.About Sophie Gerson Healthy Youth
